LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MOHAMAD CHAHINE'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. -- AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS FINAL - THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE FAC AND THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT DO NOT DIRECTLY CONFLICT REGARDING DEFENDANTS' BEING BOARD MEMBERS OR DESIGNATED BY THEIR COMPANIES TO SIT ON THE BOARD. WHILE ULTIMATELY PLAINTIFF WILL NEED TO PROVE THAT THESE DEFENDANT'S ACTED IN THEIR INDIVIDUAL CAPACITY AND NOT AS SIMPLY AGENTS OF THEIR COMPANY IN ORDER FOR INDIVIDUAL LIABILITY TO ATTACH, THIS ISSUE DOES NOT NEED TO BE DETERMINED ON A DEMURRER. MOREOVER, BECAUSE PLAINTIFF SEEKS TO HOLD DEFENDANTS LIABLE FOR THEIR INDIVIDUAL BREACHES AS BOARD MEMBERS, IT IS NOT NECESSARY THAT ALL BOARD MEMBERS BE JOINED IN THIS ACTION. THEREFORE THE DEMURRERS ARE OVERRULED ON THESE GROUNDS. DEFENDANT HUDSON ALSO ARGUES THAT PLAINTIFF'S BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY CLAIM FAILS BECAUSE ALL OF THE ALLEGED BOARD BREACHES WERE DUTIES WERE DELEGATED. HOWEVER, NOWHERE IN THE FAC DOES PLAINTIFF PLEAD THAT THE BOARD PROPERLY DELEGATED EACH OF THE DUTIES IT PURPORTEDLY BREACHED. THEREFORE, THIS IS A QUESTION OF FACT NOT PROPERLY ADDRESSED THROUGH A DEMURRER. AS TO THE APPLICATION OF THE BUSINESS JUDGMENT RULE, THIS IS AN AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE THAT CAN BE RAISED IN THE ANSWER. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED FACT SUFFICIENT TO SHOW THAT IT MAY NOT APPLY IN THIS INSTANCE. ACCORDINGLY THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED ON THIS GROUND AS WELL. IN ADDITION, THE COURT INDICATED DEFENDANTS HAVE 30 DAYS TO FILE THEIR ANSWERS. PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MARTIN BROS/MARCOWALL, INC'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. -- AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS FINAL - DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE 2ND, 6TH AND 7TH CAUSES OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT ITS CLAIMS AND MAY BRING AN ACTION FOR UNJUST ENRICHMENT. FURTHERMORE, DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER ON THE GROUNDS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO JOIN AN INDISPENSIBLE PARTY OR PARTIES IS OVERRULED. WHERE THERE IS JOINT AND SEVERABLE LIABILITY IN CONTRACT, THE PLAINTIFF IS NOT REQUIRED TO JOIN ALL OF THE PARTIES TO THE ACTION. IN ADDITION, THE COURT INDICATED DEFENDANTS HAVE 30 DAYS TO FILE THEIR ANSWERS. PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RONALD HUDSON'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT OF PLAINTIFF CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. -- AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS FINAL - THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE FAC AND THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT DO NOT DIRECTLY CONFLICT REGARDING DEFENDANTS' BEING BOARD MEMBERS OR DESIGNATED BY THEIR COMPANIES TO SIT ON THE BOARD. WHILE ULTIMATELY PLAINTIFF WILL NEED TO PROVE THAT THESE DEFENDANT'S ACTED IN THEIR INDIVIDUAL CAPACITY AND NOT AS SIMPLY AGENTS OF THEIR COMPANY IN ORDER FOR INDIVIDUAL LIABILITY TO ATTACH, THIS ISSUE DOES NOT NEED TO BE DETERMINED ON A DEMURRER. MOREOVER, BECAUSE PLAINTIFF SEEKS TO HOLD DEFENDANTS LIABLE FOR THEIR INDIVIDUAL BREACHES AS BOARD MEMBERS, IT IS NOT NECESSARY THAT ALL BOARD MEMBERS BE JOINED IN THIS ACTION. THEREFORE THE DEMURRERS ARE OVERRULED ON THESE GROUNDS. DEFENDANT HUDSON ALSO ARGUES THAT PLAINTIFF'S BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY CLAIM FAILS BECAUSE ALL OF THE ALLEGED BOARD BREACHES WERE DUTIES WERE DELEGATED. HOWEVER, NOWHERE IN THE FAC DOES PLAINTIFF PLEAD THAT THE BOARD PROPERLY DELEGATED EACH OF THE DUTIES IT PURPORTEDLY BREACHED. THEREFORE, THIS IS A QUESTION OF FACT NOT PROPERLY ADDRESSED THROUGH A DEMURRER. AS TO THE APPLICATION OF THE BUSINESS JUDGMENT RULE, THIS IS AN AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE THAT CAN BE RAISED IN THE ANSWER. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED FACT SUFFICIENT TO SHOW THAT IT MAY NOT APPLY IN THIS INSTANCE. ACCORDINGLY THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED ON THIS GROUND AS WELL. IN ADDITION, THE COURT INDICATED DEFENDANTS HAVE 30 DAYS TO FILE THEIR ANSWERS. PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

Off Calendar Jul-18-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS MOHAMAD CHAHINE AND MARTIN BROS./MARCOWALL, INC.'S JOINDER IN DEMURRERS FILED BY RONALD HUDSON AND SUPERIOR WALL SYSTEM, INC.

APPEARANCES: Karl D. Belgum, Esq. of Nixon Peabody LLP 984-8409 for pltf & opposing party; Aaron P. Rudin, Esq. of Gordon & Rees LLP 213/576-5000 for defts Ronald Hudson and Superior Wall Systems, Inc.; Robert A. Schwartz, Esq. of Schwartz & Deutsch, LLP 213/236-9494 for defts Mohamad Chahine and Martin Bros./Marcowall, Inc.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: Tentative ruling adopted as final:
Defendant Superior Wall Systems Inc.'s Demurrer -
Defendants' Demurrer to the 2nd, 6th and 7th causes of action is overruled. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to support its claims and may bring an action for Unjust enrichment.
Furthermore, Defendants' Demurrer on the grounds that Plaintiff has failed to join an indispensible party or parties is overruled. Where there is joint and severable liability in contract, the Plaintiff is not required to join all of the parties to the action.

Defendant Hudson's Demurrer -
The allegations of the FAC and the original Complaint do not directly conflict regarding defendants' being Board members or designated by their companies to sit on the Board. While ultimately Plaintiff will need to prove that these defendant's acted in their individual capacity and not as simply agents of their company in order for individual liability to attach, this issue does not need to be determined on a Demurrer. Moreover, because plaintiff seeks to hold defendants liable for their individual breaches as Board members, it is not necessary that all Board Members be joined in this action. Therefore the Demurrers are overruled on these grounds.
Defendant Hudson also argues that Plaintiff's breach of fiduciary duty claim fails because all of the alleged Board breaches were duties were delegated.
